---
title: "Pennsylvania Cudweed (Gamochaeta pensylvanica)"
slug: "gamochaeta-pensylvanica"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/475086256/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Pennsylvania Cudweed"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Magnoliopsida"
tax_order: "Asterales"
tax_family: "Asteraceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Magnoliopsida", "Asterales", "Asteraceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/475086256/medium.jpg" alt="Pennsylvania Cudweed" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/billpranty">
billpranty</a> (CC BY) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/264590286">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Magnoliopsida
-   **Order:** Asterales
-   **Family:** Asteraceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=77221)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common across Florida and found in disturbed areas, lawns, and agricultural fields.

## Natural history notes

A small, woolly herb characterized by its grayish, hairy leaves and clusters of tiny, inconspicuous flower heads. In Florida, it is commonly found in disturbed areas such as lawns, roadsides, and fields, often in sandy soils. It grows during the cooler months and can form low patches along the ground, sometimes appearing weedy.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/77221-Gamochaeta-pensylvanica)
-   [Local profile](https://mrec.ifas.ufl.edu/resources/weedsbyflowercolor/green/gamochaetapensylvanica/)
